message:
test(net): add WebSocket unassigned close code round-trip tests

Two new test cases verifying RFC 6455 §7.4.2 compliance for unassigned
close codes in the 1016–2999 range:

  - close_reason_unassigned_code_echo: verifies that receiving a frame
    with an unassigned code (1016) echoes it back correctly with the
    raw_code preserved
  - close_reason_unassigned_code_roundtrip: verifies that serializing
    an unassigned close reason through to_frame() and parsing it back
    does not panic and preserves the raw code value

These complement the existing close_reason_parse_unassigned_code_accepted
test added in the prior RFC compliance fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
